topic: Bermuda/Japan symmetry — c9 verification of ISS-1997
our_argument_summary: WIN-028 (verdict: Refuted by Data). Two folklore "disappearance" zones (Bermuda Triangle ~25°N, 71°W; Devil's Sea ~25–35°N, ~137°E) are 28° short of true antipodal longitude (15.6% deviation from 180°). NOAA, Lloyd's of London, and U.S. Coast Guard all confirm no anomalous loss rates. The dome publishes no mechanism and no tolerance for the symmetry claim; the only standard physical mechanism (agonic-line correlation) does not hold quantitatively per IGRF/WMM-2025.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No automation for this WIN — it is a qualitative geographic-folklore claim with no monitor entry.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: N/A — no live verification of the claim is attempted by dome automation.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The dome publishes no tolerance for "Geometric Symmetry CONFIRMED", which is itself an unfalsifiability problem the review flags.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: WIN-028 does not appear in monitor.py — no domain, no fetch, no comparison.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Bermuda Triangle / Devil's Sea are folklore claims, not phenomena explained by standard physics. The dome's claim is uniquely dome-flavored (symmetry on a flat disc), so it is not a relabeling.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Both folklore zones have been described in public literature for decades (Berlitz 1974 etc.) — the dome adopted them after the fact.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No derivation from dome parameters is published. The claim is purely a coincidence-observation.

change_summary: Verification cycle for ISS-1997 (decider self-apply): tldr_evidence final sentence changed from "The dome publishes no mechanism for the symmetry claim" to "The dome publishes no mechanism or tolerance for the symmetry claim". Decider commit landed via FUSE → workspace-sync rescue (universal-pusher mode) on 2026-05-13T02:05:57Z (commit a19e3115).

iss_1997_verification.verification_pattern: git show origin/main:data/wins.json | grep -qF 'no mechanism or tolerance'
iss_1997_verification.note_on_decider_failure: The closed-issues.json reports last_verify_attempt_failure_reason: "verification_pattern_failed" at 2026-05-13T02:05:50Z. This was a race condition: the decider self-apply committed to FUSE at 01:23-01:24; the verify_pending sweep at 02:05:50 checked origin/main, but the universal-pusher rescue commit (a19e3115) did not land until 02:05:57. The pattern PASSES against current origin/main as of this review (c0da52b HEAD).

holes_found.description: Open-issues / closed-issues bookkeeping inconsistency: ISS-1997 (and ISS-1998) remain in closed-issues.json with status "fixed-pending-verification" and last_verify_attempt_failure_reason "verification_pattern_failed". The patches are in origin/main and grep verifies cleanly. The next decider verify-pending sweep should flip these to status "fixed" (or "closed") on its own — this is normal pipeline lag, not a defect — but if the issue cards remain stuck on "fixed-pending-verification" after the next decider run they should be re-checked manually. Flagging for tinker awareness, not for patch.
holes_found.recommendation: No prose action required. If the issues are still stuck on fixed-pending-verification after the next decider verify-pending sweep, route to tinker for verification-pattern audit (potential race in workspace-sync push timing vs verify-pending-sweep timing).

kernel_of_truth.description: The patch directly addresses the c8-H0/H1 critique (data/wins.json:tldr_evidence) that the original "single-digit residuals (~5%)" parenthetical attributed an unpublished threshold to the dome. The current tldr ("single-digit residuals the dome treats as confirmatory at WIN-029") references a specific dome instance (WIN-029) rather than a generalized threshold, which is internally consistent with the detail_evidence at character offset ~2664-2920 (data/wins.json:WIN-028.detail_evidence) where the same WIN-029-as-internal-benchmark framing already appeared. Adding "or tolerance" to the final sentence (data/wins.json:WIN-028.tldr_evidence) directly addresses ISS-1997 (monitor/decisions/closed-issues.json:ISS-1997.description) which traced back to WIN-028.c7-H1.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The verdict (Refuted by Data, data/wins.json:WIN-028.verdict) is unchanged and unchallenged this cycle. The dome's underlying claim — that two folklore zones at "geometric symmetry" indicate physical disappearance regions — fails on three independent fronts in the detail_evidence (data/wins.json:WIN-028.detail_evidence): (a) the symmetry is quantitatively 28° off antipodal even granting the most generous latitude band; (b) the disappearance phenomenon itself is refuted by NOAA, Lloyd's, and the USCG; (c) the only published-physics mechanism (agonic-line correlation, per IGRF/WMM-2025) does not hold for either site. The TLDR polish does not change any of this — it only closes a defender attack surface on threshold-attribution language.

deeper_analysis: The ISS-1997 patch is one inserted word ("or tolerance") in the tldr final sentence. The substantive defender attack the original c7-H1 anticipated was: "The dome makes no quantitative claim, so calling the absence of a tolerance unfalsifiable is unfair." The previous tldr only flagged the mechanism gap; with this patch the tldr now flags both. This matters because TLDRs are what most readers see — non-experts encountering the dome's "CONFIRMED" framing for the first time will read the TLDR before clicking expand. A reader who only sees the TLDR now has both attack vectors armed before getting any further. The detail_evidence had already made the tolerance-gap argument in two places (data/wins.json:WIN-028.detail_evidence at "no specified tolerance" mentions at chars ~1989, ~2213, ~3777, ~3861), so the TLDR is now structurally consistent with the detail. Adversarial steelman: a defender could argue "the dome's symmetry claim is qualitative, so it makes no sense to demand a tolerance." But the dome itself uses "Geometric Symmetry CONFIRMED" — the CONFIRMED label is a binary yes/no judgment that requires a decision rule, which is what a tolerance provides. Without one, the dome cannot meaningfully fail any pair of points it picks as confirming the pattern. That is the unfalsifiability point and it stands.

One minor pipeline-bookkeeping note: ISS-1997 (and ISS-1998 below) should auto-flip from "fixed-pending-verification" to "fixed" on the next decider verify-pending sweep — the apparent failure_reason "verification_pattern_failed" was a race against the universal-pusher rescue, not an actual content defect. Recommend no further work on this WIN; pop queue_id 342.
